hard.next_step: 6/29- ARUP finally released their RFP for CLM. Intent to Respond Due 06/09/2026, RFP Questions Due 06/16/2026, Proposal Submission Deadline 07/10/2026. Michael convinced management to kick off an RFP for CLM and Zycus is included.
hard.ais_why: The risky-clause AI ask plus active engagement keeps this above AI Resistant, but no POC, no AI-tied budget, no named AI champion, and a buyer agenda still focused on CLM gap analysis keeps it at the lower end of AI Curious.
hard.competitor: Conga (Salesforce CLM); Do-nothing / enhance Conga; Coupa

north_star_verdict.headline: The champion-authored CLM RFP is genuinely live and engaged, but a 31 Jul close cannot survive a 10 Jul proposal deadline with no pricing anchored, no MSA or InfoSec pre-work, and external counsel still to redline.
north_star_verdict.math: Today 2026-07-06, close 2026-07-31 (25 days out). RFP proposal due 07/10/2026 (4 days out), then supplier selection, finalist review, pricing, legal redlines and InfoSec must all complete in ~21 days. Buyer's own mid-Feb decision target slipped ~4 months and CloseDate has been pushed five times since Mar 2026 (last 06/30 to 07/31 on 2026-06-03). Stage Shortlisted, not yet Vendor Selected or Negotiation. Last buyer touch 2026-06-24 (incoming RFP responses email). The date is optimistic, the deal is not.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Best Case (not Commit): RFP just issued, no pricing / MSA / InfoSec, realistic award likely beyond 31 Jul
evidence: SF Next_Step (6/29): RFP for CLM released, Proposal Submission Deadline 07/10/2026
SF field history: CloseDate 06/30 -> 07/31 on 2026-06-03, one of five pushes since Mar 2026
Michael Tutton (04 Feb 2026 call): 'it's still gonna be a hard sell'
Task 2026-06-24 (Mzia Sturua): 'we are sharing the responses to submitted questions with all suppliers' (formal multi-supplier RFP)

metrics.narrative: Pain is well articulated but never quantified in dollars or hours across the whole call history, a real exposure in a competitive RFP where price and value will be scored. On 16 Oct 2025 Michael Tutton described a fully manual process ('We're just reading it, memorizing, trying to remember what's not there... very manual') and the need to go '7 different places' to manage suppliers and contracts. No cost-of-inaction, cycle-time, or ROI figure has ever been put on the table. Building a savings model into the 10 Jul proposal is a ranked move.
sources: Zycus X ARUP CLM Demo 13 Oct 2025

economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority sits with a director referred to on calls only as 'mister Bevan' who mandated the Conga gap analysis and, per Michael, questioned buying the AI module standalone ('can I get the AI module without buying anything else' - 16 Oct 2025). He has never appeared on a Zycus call and is not in the Salesforce contact roles, so his exact name and title stay unconfirmed and he is not promoted here as a named executive. In this champion-authored tender the path to him runs THROUGH Michael Tutton, not around him: arming the champion with a leadership-facing value and ROI narrative is the move, not cold outreach to an unverified contact.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are clear and now codified in the RFP against Conga's gap set: side-by-side compare and redlining, clause and template library, restricted clause-level editing, external-counsel Word collaboration without portal login, AI contract discovery and Insta-review risk scoring, post-contract obligation and utilization dashboards, and D365/Merlin integration. On 13 Oct 2025 Robert Stipanovich probed licensing for external counsel and suppliers and Ondraya Watkins probed supplier risk scoring, showing the evaluation is criteria-led. The 06/16 RFP questions Steve submitted (repository scope: whether sell-side / sales contracts are in scope) show the bar is explicitly what Conga cannot do.
sources: Zycus X ARUP CLM Demo 13 Oct 2025

decision_process.narrative: Well mapped from the call sequence: champion-led internal selling, repeated CLM demos, a Bevan-mandated Conga-vs-Zycus gap analysis run with the Salesforce/Conga team over several rounds (Oct 2025 to Feb 2026), an internal executive summary Michael built, and now a formal competitive RFP that is live with multiple suppliers (Mzia Sturua, 24 Jun 2026: 'we are sharing the responses to submitted questions with all suppliers'). The RFP is both a competitive bid and a political compromise letting the director validate the champion objectively. Intent-to-respond closed 06/09, questions 06/16, proposal due 07/10; the post-proposal selection and award timeline is the critical unknown.

paper_process.narrative: The single biggest close-date blind spot. No MSA, SOW, Order Form, or template has been exchanged and no legal, redline, or InfoSec timeline has been discussed. ARUP signs today through Salesforce/Conga's DocuSign (Michael, 13 Oct 2025), and legal review runs through EXTERNAL counsel that ARUP emails Word documents to ('You send them a Word document, they email it back'), so Zycus paper will route to outside counsel, a step nothing has pre-aligned. With the proposal due 10 Jul and close 31 Jul only ~21 days remain. Pre-staging the MSA/SOW plus InfoSec and the external-counsel redline path now is what protects the date.
sources: Zycus X ARUP CLM Demo 13 Oct 2025

identify_pain.narrative: Specific and owner-attributed: a fully manual CLM run through Salesforce/Conga and Word where the team memorizes clause positions and hunts across 7 systems (Michael Tutton, 16 Oct 2025), with Conga treated as a weak repository ('it's not a very good one, but it is a repository'). Ondraya Watkins owns supplier scorecarding and post-contract risk; Arthur Moes owns supplier and minority/woman-owned business risk. Pain is confirmed and multi-owner; its dollar value is not.
sources: Zycus X ARUP CLM Demo 13 Oct 2025

champion.narrative: Michael Tutton is a strong, conviction-driven champion who found Zycus himself, openly advocates internally ('Everybody always expects me to say something Zycus' - 09 Jan 2026), and is credited in the Next Step with convincing management to launch the RFP. Ondraya Watkins is a confirmed co-champion focused on supplier risk and obligations. His limits: he admits no prior Zycus experience and forces his own objectivity, and he cannot independently move the skeptical director-level budget owner. Strong at the working level but without power-line access, so making him the economic-buyer broker with a quantified value narrative is the highest-leverage use of his strength.

competition.narrative: The live threat is incumbent Conga (Salesforce CLM), the tool the RFP exists to test, positioned as 'enhance vs replace' and backed by the director's gap-analysis mandate; do-nothing / enhance-Conga-in-place is the real fallback. Zycus wins on external-counsel Word redlining without portal login, clause library, AI contract discovery and risk scoring, post-contract dashboards, and D365/Merlin AI, the exact gap set Michael cannot close on Conga. Coupa is dormant (a Gartner peer-positioning reference only). The wedge is differentiation plus a bundled TCO that exposes the hidden cost of enhancing Conga with separate compare and sign tools.
sources: Zycus X ARUP CLM Demo 13 Oct 2025

deal_movement.summary: Live and progressing at the buyer's pace: after months of champion-led internal selling and a Conga gap analysis, ARUP formally released a CLM RFP on 6/29 with fixed dates, and the deal moved from private demos into a competitive procurement. CloseDate was last pushed 06/30 to 07/31 on 2026-06-03 (the fifth push since March).

items.change: ARUP released formal Source-to-Settle CLM RFP with fixed dates (Proposal due 07/10/2026)
Zycus submitted RFP clarification questions on repository / sell-side contract scope
ARUP shared consolidated RFP question responses to all suppliers

competitive_position.summary: Conga (Salesforce CLM) is the single strongest current threat: the director-sponsored incumbent the RFP exists to validate, positioned as lower-risk 'enhance vs replace.' Zycus wins on external-counsel Word redlining without portal login, clause library, AI risk scoring, post-contract dashboards, and D365/Merlin. Do-nothing / enhance-Conga is the active fallback to beat on differentiation and bundled price. Coupa is dormant, surfacing only as Gartner peer-positioning. The field is now a formal multi-supplier RFP, so proposal quality and price decide it.

competitors.name: Conga (Salesforce CLM)
competitors.quote: their vision is still the Conga is a repository. In my opinion, it's not a very good one, but it is a repository
competitors.how_we_win: Lead the RFP response with the gap set Conga cannot close (external-counsel Word redlining without portal login, clause library, AI contract discovery and risk scoring, post-contract dashboards, D365/Merlin) plus a bundled TCO exposing the hidden cost of enhancing Conga with separate compare and sign tools.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / enhance Conga in-place
competitors.quote: can I get the AI module without buying anything else
competitors.how_we_win: Anchor replacement value against the hidden cost of enhancing Conga plus point tools; make the totality-of-solution TCO the centerpiece.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.source: Catch up with Zycus 09 Jan 2026 (peer-positioning context)
competitors.how_we_win: Not an active bidder; reinforce the Gartner Magic Quadrant peer positioning Michael already values.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Criteria align tightly with Zycus strengths (the RFP mirrors Conga's gaps), but the deal is exposed on quantified value / TCO and on close-date-critical paper and InfoSec mechanics that have never been discussed.

best_practice_check.summary: This is a champion-led, incumbent-displacement RFP that will be scored on value and price. The three highest-leverage levers now are arming the champion to reach the budget owner, winning the RFP on the Conga gap set plus bundled TCO, and de-risking the close-date paper path.
flags: Arm Michael Tutton with a leadership-facing ROI one-pager to reach the director budget owner; the power line is his, not ours.
Win the RFP on the Conga gap set and a bundled multi-module TCO, not feature parity or a per-module price the buyer fears being nickel-and-dimed on.
Pre-stage MSA/SOW, InfoSec and the external-counsel Word redline path now; nothing on paper has been aligned for a 31 Jul close.

champion_strength.summary: Michael Tutton is a strong, conviction-driven champion credited with getting the RFP launched, but he lacks power-line access to the skeptical director budget owner and admits no prior Zycus experience. Making him the economic-buyer broker with a quantified value narrative is the highest-leverage use of his strength.
champion_strength.source: Zycus Debrief 16 Oct 2025; Catch up with Zycus 09 Jan 2026; SF Next_Step 6/29

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS is Moderate (4). Merlin / AI contract discovery, Insta-review risk scoring and negotiation genie were demoed and drew interest, but the buyer's agenda is CLM gap analysis, not AI; there is no POC, no AI-tied budget, and no named AI champion, so the agentic story is not yet a decision driver.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 4, AIS_Status Moderate. The only buyer-pulled AI ask (a screenshot of risky-clause identification) sits inside a CLM-scope action list; calls confirm interest in Merlin but no urgency, consistent with the field read rather than under- or over-rated.

items.detail: Close 31 Jul is not credible: proposal due 10 Jul, then selection, pricing, legal and InfoSec in ~21 days; CloseDate pushed five times since March.
No quantified ROI / savings case in a price-scored RFP; buyer explicitly fears being nickel-and-dimed per module.
Director budget owner ('Bevan') has never joined a Zycus call and is unverified in Salesforce; sign-off authority is not engaged.
No MSA/SOW/InfoSec pre-work; ARUP redlines via external counsel over emailed Word docs, a path nothing has aligned.
items.source: Zycus X ARUP CLM Demo 13 Oct 2025

items.detail: Do-nothing / enhance-Conga-in-place remains the director's lower-risk fallback the RFP exists to test.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is engaged, not cooling: formal RFP live, buyer-initiated dates, last buyer touch 2026-06-24. Risk is timing and value proof, not silence.

Close date slipped five times since March 2026

scope_change.from: Full Source-to-Pay suite discussion (iContract + iSupplier scorecarding/onboarding + iSource + Merlin/intake)
scope_change.to: CLM-focused RFP (iContract / Certinal CLM with Merlin)
scope_change.detail: The buyer formalized a CLM-only Source-to-Settle RFP on 6/29 rather than the full-suite evaluation explored on the Oct 2025 calls, narrowing to a phase-1 contract-management scope; a defensive, cost-contained entry point led by the director's gap-analysis mandate.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: We're the champion's chosen challenger in a live CLM RFP built to displace incumbent Conga; the only things between us and the win are a quantified value case by 10 Jul and reaching a director budget owner who has never been on a call.

win_position.text: Shortlisted caps confidence around 70; the deal earns it on a champion-authored RFP that mirrors Conga's gaps (Michael Tutton, credited in Next_Step 6/29 with launching it), but holds below cap because no economic buyer is engaged and the scope narrowed to CLM-only.

Criteria favor us: ARUP's RFP asks for external-counsel Word redlining, clause library, AI risk scoring and D365/Merlin, the exact gap set Michael said Conga cannot close (Zycus X ARUP CLM Demo, 13 Oct 2025).

The director budget owner referred to only as 'Bevan' mandated the Conga gap analysis and has never joined a Zycus call, so enhance-Conga / do-nothing remains his lower-risk fallback (Zycus Debrief, 16 Oct 2025).
No dollar ROI has ever been quantified while the buyer openly fears per-module nickel-and-diming, a real exposure in a price-scored RFP (Zycus Debrief, 16 Oct 2025).

deal_momentum.text: ARUP released a formal CLM RFP with fixed buyer-set dates on 6/29 and ran a two-way question exchange through 6/24, a genuine step up from private demos (Task, Mzia Sturua, 24 Jun 2026).
CloseDate has been pushed five times since March 2026 (last 06/30 to 07/31 on 2026-06-03) and the buyer's own mid-Feb decision target slipped roughly four months (SF field history).

customer_commitment.text: Michael Tutton convinced management to launch the RFP and openly advocates internally ('Everybody always expects me to say something Zycus', 09 Jan 2026), a strong working-level commitment.
Commitment stops below the power line: the skeptical Arthur Moes and the unseen director own the decision, and Michael admits no prior Zycus experience (Zycus Debrief, 16 Oct 2025).

deal_risk.text: A 31 Jul close cannot absorb a 10 Jul proposal deadline plus selection, pricing, legal and InfoSec with zero paper pre-work (SF Next_Step 6/29).
Legal routes through external counsel over emailed Word docs and no MSA/SOW/InfoSec path has been staged, so an award could stall at contracting (Zycus X ARUP CLM Demo, 13 Oct 2025).

customer_commitment.evidence: Strong champion but no engaged economic buyer and a skeptical decision maker.

deal_risk.evidence: Five close-date slips, no pricing or paper pre-work, unaligned external-counsel legal path.

customer_preference.evidence: Michael Tutton is a genuine advocate ('Everybody always expects me to say something Zycus', 09 Jan 2026), but the evaluation is now a formal multi-supplier RFP with no decisive Zycus-over-rival lean stated recently.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain is clear (fully manual CLM across 7 systems, 16 Oct 2025) but no ROI, savings, or cycle-time figure has ever been quantified.
